{{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしんしややないつ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:聖徳太子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしんしややないつ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:聖徳太子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしんしややないつ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:聖徳太子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005002653 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Anahoheno Shrine|lt=Anahoheno Shrine|WD=Q135039491|1=Anahoheno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県岐阜市柳津町北塚2-27', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市木曽川町玉ノ井', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:天神神社 (岐阜市柳津町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town|1=Yanaizu, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595|1=1557}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station|1=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line|1=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしんしややないつ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:聖徳太子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005002653 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Anahoheno Shrine|lt=Anahoheno Shrine|WD=Q135039491|1=Anahoheno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県岐阜市柳津町北塚2-27', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市木曽川町玉ノ井', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:天神神社 (岐阜市柳津町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town|1=Yanaizu, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595|1=1557}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station|1=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line|1=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしんしややないつ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:聖徳太子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005002653 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Anahoheno Shrine|lt=Anahoheno Shrine|WD=Q135039491|1=Anahoheno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県岐阜市柳津町北塚2-27', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市木曽川町玉ノ井', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:天神神社 (岐阜市柳津町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town|1=Yanaizu, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595|1=1557}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station|1=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line|1=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしんしややないつ}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:聖徳太子]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005002653 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Anahoheno Shrine|lt=Anahoheno Shrine|WD=Q135039491|1=Anahoheno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県岐阜市柳津町北塚2-27', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市木曽川町玉ノ井', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:天神神社 (岐阜市柳津町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town|1=Yanaizu, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595|1=1557}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station|1=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line|1=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:聖徳太子]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005002653 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Anahoheno Shrine|lt=Anahoheno Shrine|WD=Q135039491|1=Anahoheno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県岐阜市柳津町北塚2-27', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市木曽川町玉ノ井', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:天神神社 (岐阜市柳津町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town|1=Yanaizu, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595|1=1557}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station|1=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line|1=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:聖徳太子]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005002653 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Anahoheno Shrine|lt=Anahoheno Shrine|WD=Q135039491|1=Anahoheno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県岐阜市柳津町北塚2-27', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市木曽川町玉ノ井', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:天神神社 (岐阜市柳津町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town|1=Yanaizu, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595|1=1557}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station|1=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line|1=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:聖徳太子]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005002653 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Anahoheno Shrine|lt=Anahoheno Shrine|WD=Q135039491|1=Anahoheno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県岐阜市柳津町北塚2-27', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市木曽川町玉ノ井', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:天神神社 (岐阜市柳津町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town|1=Yanaizu, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595|1=1557}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station|1=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line|1=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜市の神社]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:聖徳太子]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005002653 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Anahoheno Shrine|lt=Anahoheno Shrine|WD=Q135039491|1=Anahoheno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県岐阜市柳津町北塚2-27', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市木曽川町玉ノ井', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:天神神社 (岐阜市柳津町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town|1=Yanaizu, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595|1=1557}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station|1=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line|1=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:聖徳太子]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005002653 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Anahoheno Shrine|lt=Anahoheno Shrine|WD=Q135039491|1=Anahoheno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県岐阜市柳津町北塚2-27', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市木曽川町玉ノ井', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:天神神社 (岐阜市柳津町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town|1=Yanaizu, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595|1=1557}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station|1=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line|1=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:Prince Shōtoku]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005002653 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Anahoheno Shrine|lt=Anahoheno Shrine|WD=Q135039491|1=Anahoheno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県岐阜市柳津町北塚2-27', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市木曽川町玉ノ井', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:天神神社 (岐阜市柳津町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town|1=Yanaizu, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595|1=1557}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station|1=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line|1=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Prince Shōtoku]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4200005002653 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Anahoheno Shrine|lt=Anahoheno Shrine|WD=Q135039491|1=Anahoheno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県岐阜市柳津町北塚2-27', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市木曽川町玉ノ井', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:天神神社 (岐阜市柳津町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11443286}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tenjin Shrine |画像=[[File:Tenjin Temple in Yanaizu.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}} Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenjin Shrine''' (てんじんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yanaizu-chō (former {{ill|lt=Yanaizu, Gifu|Q8048244|WD=Q8048244|lt=Yanaizu Town|1=Yanaizu, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hashima District|Q615068|WD=Q615068|1=Hashima District, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Gifu|Q45798|WD=Q45798|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called Yanaizu Tenjin Shrine. It is Anahobe Shrine of Haguri District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (there is also a theory that Anahobe Shrine is {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q11634949|WD=Q11634949|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} in Ichinomiya, Aichi). The current Tenjin Shrine is a merged shrine of Anahobe Shrine, Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and Naka-Tenjin Shrine. === Outline === *The founding time of Anahobe Shrine is unclear, but one account says it was founded in the 6th century as the guardian shrine of Taishi-dera (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōtaku-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}), founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. The enshrined deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}}. The location is said to have been about 500 m south of the current Tenjin Shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called “Akata Tenjin” and was the guardian shrine of Kōtaku-ji, but became independent through {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. *Kita-Tenjin Shrine was founded in {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|WD=Q6595|1=1557}} ({{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|WD=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}} 3) by descendants of Minamoto no Yorizane (or Minamoto no Yorisada), enshrining an image of Sugawara no Michizane made by him; its deity was {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. Naka-Tenjin Shrine has an unknown foundation date. *In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), Anahobe Shrine and Naka-Tenjin Shrine were merged into Kita-Tenjin Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1954|Q18581|WD=Q18581|1=1954}} (Shōwa 29) it was renamed Tenjin Shrine. On August 17, {{ill|lt=1965|Q2650|WD=Q2650|1=1965}} (Shōwa 40), it was designated by the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a prefectural shinto association chief-visit designated shrine ({{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}), designation No. Kin-63{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=91}}{{sfn|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017|p=379}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|WD=Q10940685|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame no Hohi}} *{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Location === *Yanaizu-chō Kitazuka 2-27, Gifu, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)|Q8048245|WD=Q8048245|lt=Yanaizu Station|1=Yanaizu Station (Gifu)}} on the {{ill|lt=Takehana Line|Q901442|WD=Q901442|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line|1=Meitetsu Takehana Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|editor=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|title=Directory of Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2017|ref={{sfnref|Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|2017}}}} * {{cite book|和書|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}}|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenjin Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Prince Shōtoku]]
